paper_summary.main_contribution: The paper demonstrates that selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) biosynthesized using Fenugreek seeds extract are effective in controlling Fusarium spp. infection in tomatoes, extending shelf-life, and maintaining fruit quality without harming normal cells.
paper_summary.narrative: The paper begins by confirming the successful biosynthesis of crystalline SeNPs using XRD and UV-VIS spectroscopy (Fig. 1). The characterization of SeNPs using EDX, TEM, and FTIR (Fig. 2) shows their elemental composition, morphology, and functional groups. The antifungal activity of SeNPs against Fusarium spp. is demonstrated through inhibition zones and MIC/MFC values (Table 1). The efficacy of SeNPs in preventing fungal growth on tomatoes is visually confirmed (Fig. 3), and microscopic examination shows morphological changes in fungal mycelium (Fig. 4). The treatment of infected tomatoes with SeNPs reduces infection signs and maintains fruit quality (Fig. 5). Cytotoxicity tests indicate SeNPs are safe for normal cells (Fig. 6). The preparation of tomatoes for the study is illustrated (Fig. 7), and the impact of SeNPs on chemical and physical properties is shown (Fig. 9). The table highlights the extended shelf-life and reduced disease progression in treated tomatoes (Table 2).
paper_summary.limitations_noted: The study lacks statistical analysis in some figures and does not provide detailed information on the long-term effects of SeNPs on tomato quality and safety.
